Title: (Z)-3-Hexenol integrates drought and cold stress signaling by activating abscisic acid glucosylation in tea plants

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: C. sinensis var. sinensis cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: C. sinensis var. sinensis cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a key reductive step leading to formation of (Z)-3-hexenol in tea plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Integrates cold and drought stress responses and enhances drought tolerance in tea plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissue under cold and drought context; reduced by antisense oligonucleotide silencing
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress (-5 °C), combined cold and drought stress, volatile-mediated neighbor interaction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes (Z)-3-hexenol production and thereby enhances drought tolerance and ABA accumulation in neighboring tea plants under combined cold and drought stress; silencing reduces both
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsADH2 is described as a vital enzyme for catalyzing the reduction leading to (Z)-3-hexenol production in tea plants. 2. Silencing CsADH2 reduced (Z)-3-hexenol emission. 3. CsADH2-silenced plants showed more severe drought damage under combined cold and drought stress. 4. Neighbors of CsADH2-silenced emitters had reduced moisture and ABA content, supporting a causal role of CsADH2-dependent (Z)-3-hexenol in stress integration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: AsODN gene silencing; GC-MS volatile quantification; drought phenotype assessment; relative moisture measurement; ABA quantification in neighboring plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing increased drought damage under combined stress; no broader growth tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior work for CsADH2 enzymatic role; in this study the causal evidence is from silencing and metabolite/phenotype changes.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: C. sinensis var. sinensis cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: C. sinensis var. sinensis cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA homeostasis and glycosylation; volatile glycoside metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates abscisic acid to form ABA-GE and also glucosylates (Z)-3-hexenol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates drought tolerance, stomatal regulation, and ROS control
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by substrate accumulation such as (Z)-3-hexenol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after (Z)-3-hexenol exposure under drought stress; reduced by AsODN silencing
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous (Z)-3-hexenol exposure; drought stress; combined cold and drought signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA homeostasis, ABA/ABA-GE accumulation, and drought tolerance in (Z)-3-hexenol-exposed tea plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsUGT85A53 is a dual-function glycosyltransferase acting on both (Z)-3-hexenol and ABA. 2. (Z)-3-hexenol stimulates CsUGT85A53 expression. 3. Silencing CsUGT85A53 increased water loss and stress damage and lowered Fv/Fm, ABA, and ABA-GE after (Z)-3-hexenol exposure. 4. The results support CsUGT85A53-mediated ABA glycosylation as central to integration of cold and drought stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: AsODN gene silencing; RT-qPCR; ABA and ABA-GE quantification; chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm); DAB/NBT ROS staining; water loss measurement
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for ABA homeostasis in the context of (Z)-3-hexenol-induced drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing compromises drought tolerance and increases ROS damage; no broader developmental penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes feedback activation of ABA biosynthesis-related genes such as NCED following altered glycosylation/homeostasis; this is discussed mechanistically but not directly proven here.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: C. sinensis var. sinensis cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: C. sinensis var. sinensis cv. Shuchazao
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Volatile ester; green leaf volatile derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Not shown to mediate drought tolerance in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by (Z)-3-hexenol exposure; reduced by AsODN silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes conversion of (Z)-3-hexenol to (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate, but silencing does not alter (Z)-3-hexenol-induced drought tolerance, ABA, ABA-GE, or soluble sugar accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCHAT was identified as a (Z)-3-hexenol acetyltransferase with in vitro enzymatic activity producing (Z)-3-hexenyl acetate. 2. CsCHAT expression is induced after (Z)-3-hexenol exposure. 3. Silencing CsCHAT did not change drought tolerance or ABA/ABA-GE accumulation after (Z)-3-hexenol exposure. 4. Therefore, acetylation is not the main route mediating stress integration in this system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate gene expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; GC-MS product detection; AsODN gene silencing; drought physiology and metabolite assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear metabolic or growth tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although experimentally characterized, CsCHAT functions mainly as a negative/control branch in the mechanistic dissection and is not the decisive mediator of drought tolerance.
